subject: Databases
title: Tables Are the Foundation
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Tables Are the Foundation.

hook.text: Everything in a relational database lives in tables. No tables, no SQL.

buildup.text: A table has a name, columns with types, and rows of data. Think of a spreadsheet tab with strict rules.

discovery.text: CREATE TABLE users (id INT, name TEXT, email TEXT) — that's all it takes to define one.

twist.text: Tables enforce structure. Every row has the same columns. No surprises, no missing fields.

climax.text: Good table design is the foundation of a good database. Get it wrong and queries suffer forever.

punchline.text: Tables: rigid by design. That's the strength.

quiz.question: Why is table design so important in relational databases?
options: It forms the foundation — poor design leads to difficult and slow queries
Tables are the only way to display data visually
Table design only matters for very large databases
